reasoning: The informal statement claims ℚ has no proper subgroups of finite index. The Lean theorem states that for any AddSubgroup H of ℚ with H ≠ ⊤ (i.e., any proper subgroup), H.index = 0. In Mathlib, AddSubgroup.index is defined as Nat.card (G ⧸ H), which equals 0 precisely when the index is infinite (confirmed by `index_eq_zero_iff_infinite`). Thus H.index = 0 is equivalent to "H does not have finite index". The universal quantification over all proper subgroups, the type AddSubgroup ℚ (standard for additive subgroups of ℚ), and the encoding of infinite index as 0 all correctly capture the intended meaning. The statement is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
